summary: Fighter subsystem slice split from PR #2617. It touched nine src/melee/ft files with small matching-oriented cleanups, typed access improvements, helper reuse, stack/local-order tweaks, and ftmaterial data additions. The decomp-dev report for this slice credited 6 new matches and 11 unmatched-item improvements, including full matches for ftCo_800D730C, ftCo_80093BC0, ftCo_800978D4, ftMaterial_800BF260, ftMaterial_800BF534, and ftmaterial .data.

key_files.role: Fighter common matching cleanups: removed temporary gravity pointer, reused ftCo_800AD54C_inline0 command helper, reused ftCo_800A1AB4 distance helper, and replaced M2C_FIELD access with Fighter_804D64FC->x20.
Matched ftCo_800D730C and improved fn_800D8EC8 through declaration ordering, an extern float for vel.z, more specific Link catch attrs typing, and direct fp->fv.lk.xC use.
evidence_refs: text_corpus.jsonl decomp-dev report: ftCo_800D730C 98.87% to 100.00%, fn_800D8EC8 98.74% to 99.99%

key_files.role: Expanded rotation-matrix construction in ftCo_DamageIce_Init using HSD_JObjGetRotation, quaternion flag checks, HSD_MkRotationMtx or HSD_MtxQuat, and PSMTXTranspose.
Inlined top joint access into efAsync_Spawn call and preserved PAD_STACK.
Small fighter-access and stack-padding changes; GET_FIGHTER replaced gobj->user_data in ftCo_80093BC0.
evidence_refs: text_corpus.jsonl decomp-dev report: ftCo_80093BC0 99.91% to 100.00%, ftCo_80092908 99.79% to 99.92%

key_files.role: Introduced a typed co_attrs.xBC pointer and PAD_STACK in Kirby/Yoshi egg hurtbox setup.
Added a nested fp2 alias around ftCommon_8007D5D4 in Nana/Popo special collision inline.
Adjusted player-log call argument order, local declaration order, and float-zero test shape in ftColl_80077C60.
Added static HSD_TevDesc and HSD_TECnst data before force_active, contributing to full ftmaterial .data and two ftmaterial function matches.
evidence_refs: text_corpus.jsonl decomp-dev report: ftmaterial .data 73.58% to 100.00%, ftMaterial_800BF260 and ftMaterial_800BF534 to 100.00%

what_changed: The PR was a path-scoped fighter slice from PR #2617, limited to src/melee/ft.
Changed nine files with 75 additions and 43 deletions according to PR metadata.
Replaced some generated-looking temporary pointer and raw typed-access forms with direct field or typed global field access, notably fp->co_attrs.grav and Fighter_804D64FC->x20.
Collapsed an explicit sequence of CpuCmd calls in ftCo_800AB224 into ftCo_800AD54C_inline0.
Reused ftCo_800A1AB4 for fighter-to-target distance checks instead of open-coded dx/dy/sqrtf logic.
Expanded DamageIce joint rotation matrix setup into explicit HSD_JObjGetRotation, quaternion flag handling, and matrix transpose calls.
Added or adjusted PAD_STACK and declaration/local ordering in several functions.
Added ftmaterial static HSD_TevDesc and HSD_TECnst data records.
Per decomp-dev, the slice produced 6 new matches and 11 improvements in unmatched items.

smart_moves.text: Kept the slice path-scoped to src/melee/ft after splitting from a larger matching branch, which made review and reporting narrower.
Reused existing helpers where the diff shows equivalent local operations, such as replacing a CpuCmd call sequence with ftCo_800AD54C_inline0 and open-coded distance math with ftCo_800A1AB4.
Used typed accesses for known fighter data rather than keeping M2C_FIELD or unnecessary temporary pointer indirection.
Included automated slice-level decomp-dev reporting with concrete item-level before/after percentages.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a global or struct type is known, replacing M2C_FIELD or pointer temporaries with the typed field can both improve readability and support matching.
Look for existing local helper functions before preserving repeated command sequences or open-coded math.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftCo_800AB224 command sequence replaced by ftCo_800AD54C_inline0
raw_diff: ftCo_800AF290 sqrtf distance replaced by ftCo_800A1AB4

decomp_lessons.lesson: Small declaration-order, stack-padding, and local-lifetime changes can be part of matching work, but the evidence here supports them only as narrow tactics tied to the reported functions, not as general style.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: PAD_STACK changes in ftCo_Guard.c and ftkirbyyoshiegg.c
raw_diff: local declaration/order changes in ftCo_Attack100.c, ftCo_DamageIce.c, ftcoll.c

decomp_lessons.lesson: Data additions may be necessary for a matching PR when automated reports identify concrete data-section closure, as with ftmaterial .data in this slice.

naming_conventions.text: Use subsystem-specific typedefs when the role is known: fn_800D8EC8 changed attrs from struct ftLk_DatAttrs* to ftCo_LinkCatchAttrs*.
Short local aliases remained mechanical and role-based rather than speculative: xBCp points at fp->co_attrs.xBC, fp2 is a narrow alias used only around a call-shape tweak.
Address-style names were retained for unresolved symbols and statics such as ftCo_804D9018, ftMaterial_803C69D0, and ftMaterial_803C6A44.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Declaration reordering and delayed initialization of locals.
examples: ftCo_800D730C moves Fighter* fp and x2d0 initialization below unused declarations

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: PAD_STACK size adjustments or additions for local stack shape.
Use helper calls to recover likely authored source and improve match.
examples: ftCo_800A1AB4 replaces open-coded target distance calculation

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use concrete static data definitions for ftmaterial data matching.

review_feedback.author: decomp-dev[bot]
review_feedback.text: Report for GALE01 credited matched code +1280 bytes and matched data +336 bytes, with 6 new matches and 11 improvements in unmatched items.
No human review comments or review records were present in the loaded PR slice.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs touching ftCo_800AD54C_inline0 or CpuCmd sequence cleanup in ftCo_0A01.c
Search for remaining M2C_FIELD(Fighter_804D64FC, float**, 0x20) or similar typed global field opportunities
Search for PAD_STACK changes in fighter PRs that have decomp-dev item-level evidence
Find later changes to ftMaterial_803C69D0 or ftMaterial_803C6A44 to confirm data ownership and naming
Search for ftCo_LinkCatchAttrs usage around fn_800D8EC8 and Link hookshot attrs

evidence_refs: metadata_json: PR identity, state, changed files, counts
human_pr_text.md: scope, split rationale, preliminary report, local checks
raw_diff: complete diff excerpt for nine changed files
text_corpus.jsonl: decomp-dev report with new matches and improvements
notes: The raw PR JSON loaded file is truncated, but the needed identity fields, body, and metadata are also present in untruncated metadata and extracted text files.
No human review feedback is available in this slice.
The PR body says per-PR CI/decomp-dev reporting is authoritative once complete; the loaded decomp-dev issue comment provides item-level slice results.
The preliminary report in the PR body refers to the full unsplit source branch, so this postmortem treats those numbers as context rather than slice-specific validation.
Matching tactics such as PAD_STACK and local aliasing are recorded as tactics with report evidence, not promoted as broad style guidance.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Typed fighter data access can replace M2C_FIELD and temporary pointer bridges
accepted_candidate_records.text: In this fighter slice, replacing M2C_FIELD(Fighter_804D64FC, float**, 0x20)[fp->kind] with Fighter_804D64FC->x20[fp->kind] and removing a temp pointer to fp->co_attrs.grav coincided with reported improvements in ftCo_0A01 functions.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Existing helpers can recover cleaner fighter source shape
accepted_candidate_records.text: The diff replaces an explicit CpuCmd sequence with ftCo_800AD54C_inline0 and an open-coded distance calculation with ftCo_800A1AB4, supporting helper search as a matching tactic in ftCommon.

source_update_candidates.title: PR #2620 was an src/melee/ft path-scoped split from PR #2617
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2620 was split from #2617 by directory/subsystem and scoped to src/melee/ft, touching fighter common, Kirby, Nana, ftcoll, and ftmaterial files.
source_update_candidates.reason: May help future path-scoped PR lookup and historical slice reconstruction.

source_update_candidates.title: ftmaterial data additions in PR #2620 closed ftmaterial .data
source_update_candidates.text: The slice added static HSD_TevDesc ftMaterial_803C69D0 and HSD_TECnst ftMaterial_803C6A44 in src/melee/ft/ftmaterial.c; decomp-dev reported main/melee/ft/ftmaterial .data moving from 73.58% to 100.00%.
source_update_candidates.reason: Potential source-owned path fact about data ownership and matching history; curator should verify against current symbols/source before promotion.

source_update_candidates.title: Do not generalize PAD_STACK/local-order tweaks without narrow evidence
source_update_candidates.text: This PR contains PAD_STACK, declaration-order, and local-alias tweaks tied to item-level report gains. If added to standards, it should reinforce that these are narrow matching tactics requiring verifier evidence, not normal source style.
source_update_candidates.reason: Consistent with existing matching-tactics-need-evidence standard; curator may decide whether this PR is useful as an example rather than a new rule.

rejection_notes: Do not treat the unsplit source-branch preliminary report as the slice's exact result; use the decomp-dev issue comment for slice-level item evidence.
Do not infer reviewer intent or human approval rationale; no human review comments were loaded.
Do not promote PAD_STACK or fp2-style aliasing as general decomp style from this PR alone.
Do not infer semantic names for address-style data symbols such as ftMaterial_803C69D0 or ftCo_804D9018 beyond what the diff shows.
